channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: United Nations OCHA Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator (P-2) Job in The Hague, Netherlands
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) has officially opened applications for the position of Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator (P-2) based in The Hague, Netherlands. This is an outstanding international career opportunity for communication professionals who are passionate about humanitarian action, digital storytelling, advocacy, public engagement, and global impact. […]

The post United Nations OCHA Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator (P-2) Job in The Hague, Netherlands appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) has officially opened applications for the position of Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator (P-2) based in The Hague, Netherlands. This is an outstanding international career opportunity for communication professionals who are passionate about humanitarian action, digital storytelling, advocacy, public engagement, and global impact.

The position is part of the Partnerships Communications Unit within the Public Engagement Section of OCHA’s Communications Branch. Successful candidates will support communication strategies aimed at strengthening advocacy, visibility, fundraising efforts, and public engagement for humanitarian causes worldwide.

This United Nations career opportunity offers professionals the chance to contribute directly to humanitarian coordination and emergency response communication efforts while working in a globally respected international organization.

About the United Nations OCHA

The United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, commonly known as OCHA, is part of the United Nations Secretariat responsible for coordinating humanitarian responses during emergencies and disasters.

OCHA works to:

  • Bring humanitarian actors together
  • Ensure coordinated emergency responses
  • Advocate for people affected by crises
  • Promote preparedness and prevention
  • Support sustainable humanitarian solutions
  • Mobilize humanitarian assistance globally

The organization plays a critical role in ensuring that aid reaches vulnerable populations efficiently and effectively during humanitarian emergencies.

Overview of the Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator Position

The Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator role is designed for professionals with expertise in communication strategy, digital engagement, advocacy campaigns, storytelling, content creation, and public outreach.

The selected candidate will support OCHA’s communication objectives by helping design and implement impactful communication campaigns that increase visibility and strengthen engagement with stakeholders and audiences worldwide.

Job Details

  • Position Title: Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator
  • Job Opening ID: 276433
  • Job Network: Public Information and Conference Management
  • Job Family: Public Information
  • Category and Level: Professional and Higher Categories, P-2
  • Duty Station: The Hague, Netherlands
  • Department/Office: Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA)
  • Date Posted: 29 April 2026
  • Application Deadline: 28 May 2026

Key Responsibilities of the Position

The Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator will undertake a broad range of communication and advocacy-related responsibilities.

These responsibilities may include:

1. Communication Strategy Support

The selected candidate will:

  • Assist in developing communication strategies
  • Support advocacy and visibility campaigns
  • Promote humanitarian messaging effectively
  • Help strengthen fundraising communication efforts

2. Digital Content Creation

Responsibilities may include:

  • Producing multimedia and digital content
  • Developing engaging storytelling materials
  • Supporting social media communication
  • Creating audience-centered public information materials

3. Campaign Monitoring and Analytics

The role may involve:

  • Monitoring communication campaigns
  • Evaluating digital engagement metrics
  • Tracking communication performance
  • Analyzing outreach effectiveness

4. Public Engagement and Advocacy

The candidate will support:

  • Advocacy initiatives
  • Outreach campaigns
  • Internal communication activities
  • Stakeholder engagement efforts

5. Humanitarian Communication Support

The position contributes to:

  • Crisis communication
  • Public awareness initiatives
  • Humanitarian visibility campaigns
  • Emergency communication coordination

Why This UN Job Opportunity Is Important

Working with OCHA provides professionals with the opportunity to directly contribute to global humanitarian efforts and international development initiatives.

This role is particularly valuable for communication professionals seeking:

  • International work experience
  • Exposure to humanitarian operations
  • Career growth within the United Nations system
  • Opportunities to influence global audiences
  • Experience in strategic communication and advocacy

Professionals in this position will work in a multicultural environment alongside international experts committed to humanitarian action and sustainable global solutions.

Benefits of Working with the United Nations

Although the full compensation package is not detailed in the announcement, UN Professional Category positions generally offer several benefits, including:

  • Competitive international salary
  • Health insurance and benefits
  • International career development opportunities
  • Multicultural work environment
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Potential mobility within the UN system
  • Meaningful global impact through humanitarian work

Work Location: The Hague, Netherlands

The selected candidate will be based in The Hague, one of the world’s leading international cities and home to numerous global organizations, international courts, and diplomatic institutions.

Working in The Hague offers:

  • An international professional environment
  • Access to global policy and humanitarian networks
  • Exposure to international governance systems
  • High-quality professional development opportunities

Who Should Apply?

This role is ideal for:

  • Communication professionals
  • Journalists and media practitioners
  • Public relations specialists
  • Digital storytellers
  • Social media strategists
  • Advocacy and outreach professionals
  • Humanitarian communication experts
  • International affairs communicators

Candidates passionate about humanitarian action, public engagement, and impactful storytelling are strongly encouraged to apply.

Skills and Qualities Needed

Successful applicants are likely to possess:

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Digital communication expertise
  • Content creation and storytelling abilities
  • Analytical and campaign monitoring skills
  • Creativity and innovation
  • Ability to work in multicultural environments
  • Interest in humanitarian affairs and international cooperation

Application Deadline

Deadline: 28 May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid last-minute technical issues and to ensure all required documentation is properly completed.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Visit the official United Nations Careers portal
  2. Search for Job Opening ID: 276433
  3. Complete the online application
  4. Upload required documents and credentials
  5. Submit the application before the deadline

Applicants should carefully review:

  • Education requirements
  • Work experience criteria
  • Language requirements
  • Competency expectations

before submitting their applications.

Apply HERE

Why You Should Consider This Opportunity

The United Nations OCHA Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator position is more than just a communications job. It is an opportunity to become part of a global mission focused on saving lives, supporting vulnerable communities, and strengthening humanitarian responses worldwide.

For professionals interested in combining communication expertise with meaningful international impact, this role offers a rare chance to build a globally recognized career while contributing to humanitarian action on an international scale.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global job oppportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post United Nations OCHA Associate Communications Officer / Content Creator (P-2) Job in The Hague, Netherlands appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNESCO ICT in Education Prize 2026: Win US$25,000 for Innovative AI Projects Transforming Creativity and Critical Thinking in Education
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming the future of education, reshaping the way learners think, create, solve problems, and interact with knowledge. As AI tools become increasingly powerful in generating content, solving complex problems, and automating intellectual tasks, educators worldwide are facing an urgent question: How can education preserve and strengthen human creativity, imagination, and critical […]

The post UNESCO ICT in Education Prize 2026: Win US$25,000 for Innovative AI Projects Transforming Creativity and Critical Thinking in Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming the future of education, reshaping the way learners think, create, solve problems, and interact with knowledge. As AI tools become increasingly powerful in generating content, solving complex problems, and automating intellectual tasks, educators worldwide are facing an urgent question: How can education preserve and strengthen human creativity, imagination, and critical thinking in the age of AI?

To address this important challenge, the UNESCO has officially opened nominations for the 2026 edition of the UNESCO King Hamad Bin Isa Al-Khalifa Prize for the Use of ICT in Education. The prestigious international award seeks to recognize outstanding projects that demonstrate how artificial intelligence can enhance—not replace—human thinking and creativity in learning environments.

The 2026 edition is themed:

“Reimagining Creativity and Critical Thinking with Artificial Intelligence”

This global opportunity aims to spotlight innovative educational projects that use AI responsibly while empowering learners and teachers to strengthen imagination, reasoning, discernment, creativity, and problem-solving skills.

The prize offers international recognition, a diploma, and a financial reward of US$25,000 for each of the two selected winners.

About the UNESCO ICT in Education Prize

The UNESCO ICT in Education Prize was established to celebrate groundbreaking initiatives that effectively use information and communication technologies (ICT) to improve education systems and learning outcomes globally.

For the 2026 edition, UNESCO is focusing specifically on how AI can be integrated into education without diminishing the human capacity for critical thought and creativity.

According to UNESCO, education has a central role to play in ensuring that AI serves as a tool for expanding human agency rather than becoming a substitute for independent thinking. While AI systems can now compose essays, generate ideas, solve equations, and produce information instantly, there is growing concern that learners may become overly dependent on these technologies.

The prize therefore seeks projects that encourage learners to:

  • Think independently
  • Explore creatively
  • Question information critically
  • Solve problems innovatively
  • Engage thoughtfully with AI systems
  • Preserve imagination and originality

Why This UNESCO Prize Matters

Artificial intelligence is becoming deeply integrated into classrooms, universities, online learning platforms, and informal education spaces worldwide. However, many educators and policymakers are concerned about the “flattening effect” AI could have on human creativity and intellectual development if used improperly.

This UNESCO initiative highlights the importance of balancing technological advancement with human-centered learning.

Winning projects are expected to demonstrate how AI can:

  1. Support creative learning experiences
  2. Encourage analytical reasoning
  3. Strengthen imagination and curiosity
  4. Promote ethical and thoughtful AI use
  5. Complement human intelligence rather than replace it

The award also provides a platform for global visibility, helping successful initiatives inspire educators and institutions around the world.

Prize Benefits

Selected winners of the UNESCO ICT in Education Prize 2026 will receive:

1. Financial Reward

Each of the two winners will receive:

  • US$25,000

2. International Recognition

Winning projects will gain:

  • Global exposure through UNESCO platforms
  • International credibility and prestige
  • Recognition from leading education and AI experts

3. Official Diploma

Awardees will receive an official UNESCO diploma recognizing their achievement and contribution to education innovation.

4. Award Ceremony Participation

Winners will be recognized during an international award ceremony scheduled for September 2026.

Theme Focus: Reimagining Creativity and Critical Thinking with AI

The 2026 edition specifically seeks projects that demonstrate meaningful and innovative engagement with AI systems in educational settings.

UNESCO is particularly interested in initiatives showing how:

  • Teachers use AI to improve creativity-based learning
  • Students use AI responsibly to enhance learning outcomes
  • AI tools support deeper thinking and inquiry
  • Educational systems preserve human reasoning and imagination
  • Learning environments encourage originality despite AI automation

Projects can come from:

  • Formal education settings
  • Non-formal learning spaces
  • Informal education environments
  • Community learning initiatives
  • Digital education ecosystems

Who Can Apply?

Applications are open to a broad range of participants, including:

  • Individuals
  • Schools and universities
  • NGOs
  • Research institutions
  • Educational organizations
  • Innovation hubs
  • Other entities working in education and AI

However, applicants must meet the following condition:

Project Requirement

The submitted project must:

  • Already be operational
  • Have existed for at least one year
  • Align with the 2026 theme on creativity, critical thinking, and AI

Important Nomination Requirement

One of the most important aspects of the application process is that self-nominations are not allowed.

Applications must be submitted through:

  • A UNESCO National Commission from a Member State, or
  • An NGO officially partnered with UNESCO

UNESCO encourages applicants to contact the relevant National Commission or partner NGO early in the application process.

Additionally:

  • Governments of UNESCO Member States
  • NGOs in official partnership with UNESCO

may nominate up to three projects.

Selection Process

An international jury will review all eligible nominations and evaluate projects based on:

  • Innovation
  • Educational impact
  • Relevance to the 2026 theme
  • Sustainability
  • Effectiveness in promoting creativity and critical thinking through AI

The final winners will be selected by the Director-General of UNESCO following the jury’s recommendations.

Deadline for Applications

Nomination Deadline:

29 May 2026 (23:59 CEST)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin the nomination process early because coordination with National Commissions or UNESCO partner NGOs may require additional time.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Prepare documentation about their project
  2. Ensure the project aligns with the 2026 theme
  3. Contact a UNESCO National Commission or official UNESCO partner NGO
  4. Request nomination support
  5. Submit the application through the authorized nominating body

Official Links and Contact Information

For full application details and nomination guidelines, visit the official UNESCO Prize webpage and UNESCO website.

For inquiries:

  • Email: ictprize@unesco.org

This opportunity represents a major global platform for innovators shaping the future of AI in education responsibly and creatively.

Whether you are an educator, institution, or organization developing transformative AI learning experiences, the UNESCO ICT in Education Prize 2026 offers a unique opportunity to gain international recognition and contribute to the future of human-centered education.

Discover more global award opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post UNESCO ICT in Education Prize 2026: Win US$25,000 for Innovative AI Projects Transforming Creativity and Critical Thinking in Education appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AfricaNenda Foundation Paid Program Support Internship 2026: Remote Opportunity for Young Professionals Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
AfricaNenda Foundation has announced an exciting paid internship opportunity for young professionals interested in contributing to the growth of inclusive instant payment systems across Africa. The organization is currently recruiting a Program Support Intern to join its Projects Team in a fully remote, full-time role lasting six months. This opportunity is ideal for ambitious young […]

The post AfricaNenda Foundation Paid Program Support Internship 2026: Remote Opportunity for Young Professionals Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
AfricaNenda Foundation has announced an exciting paid internship opportunity for young professionals interested in contributing to the growth of inclusive instant payment systems across Africa. The organization is currently recruiting a Program Support Intern to join its Projects Team in a fully remote, full-time role lasting six months.

This opportunity is ideal for ambitious young Africans seeking hands-on experience in digital financial inclusion, project coordination, payments ecosystems, and development-focused initiatives that are transforming financial systems across the continent.

The application deadline is 29 May 2026.

About AfricaNenda Foundation

AfricaNenda Foundation is a leading organization focused on accelerating the growth of inclusive instant payment systems throughout Africa. The foundation works with governments, financial institutions, fintech companies, regulators, and development partners to improve access to safe, affordable, and interoperable digital payments.

Its mission is centered on advancing financial inclusion by supporting payment infrastructure that enables individuals and businesses to send and receive money instantly and securely.

AfricaNenda plays a major role in helping African countries build stronger digital economies while supporting economic empowerment, innovation, and cross-border financial integration.

About the Program Support Internship

The AfricaNenda Foundation Program Support Internship is designed to support the Projects Team in implementing initiatives related to inclusive payment systems and financial inclusion across Africa.

The internship offers participants the opportunity to work in an international and impact-driven environment while gaining practical experience in programme coordination, project management support, stakeholder engagement, and operational support.

This is a paid internship and will be conducted remotely, making it accessible to qualified candidates across different African countries.

Internship Details

  • Organization: AfricaNenda Foundation
  • Position: Program Support Intern
  • Internship Type: Paid Internship
  • Work Mode: Remote
  • Duration: 6 Months
  • Employment Type: Full-Time
  • Application Deadline: 29 May 2026

Why This Internship Matters

Digital payment systems are becoming increasingly important for economic development, entrepreneurship, trade, and financial inclusion in Africa. Instant payment systems help individuals and businesses transact more efficiently, reduce dependence on cash, and improve access to financial services.

Through this internship, participants will gain exposure to:

  1. Financial Inclusion Projects
    • Understanding how payment systems support underserved populations
    • Learning about inclusive financial ecosystems
  2. Digital Payments and Fintech Ecosystems
    • Exposure to payment infrastructure initiatives
    • Understanding instant payment systems in African markets
  3. International Development Work
    • Collaborating with experts across multiple countries
    • Supporting continent-wide development initiatives
  4. Remote Professional Experience
    • Working in a multicultural and international remote environment
    • Building communication and coordination skills
  5. Project and Programme Support
    • Assisting project teams with coordination and implementation activities
    • Supporting reporting and administrative processes

Benefits of the AfricaNenda Internship

Selected interns may benefit from:

  • Paid internship experience
  • Full-time remote work flexibility
  • Exposure to Africa-wide financial inclusion projects
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Experience with international development organizations
  • Skills development in programme support and coordination
  • Experience in digital finance and payment systems
  • Opportunity to contribute to impactful projects across Africa

Who Should Apply?

This internship is suitable for young professionals and graduates interested in:

  • Financial inclusion
  • Fintech and digital payments
  • Project management
  • International development
  • Economic development
  • Innovation ecosystems
  • Public policy
  • Technology-driven development initiatives

Candidates passionate about transforming Africa’s financial systems and supporting inclusive economic growth are especially encouraged to apply.

Skills and Qualities That May Strengthen Applications

Although detailed eligibility requirements were not fully listed in the announcement, strong candidates are likely to demonstrate:

  • Excellent communication skills
  • Organizational and coordination abilities
  • Interest in digital finance and innovation
  • Strong teamwork and collaboration skills
  • Ability to work independently in remote settings
  • Research and administrative support skills
  • Interest in African development and financial inclusion

Why Young Africans Should Consider This Opportunity

Africa’s digital economy is growing rapidly, and payment systems are at the center of economic transformation. Internships like this provide early-career professionals with valuable exposure to the future of finance and technology on the continent.

Working with AfricaNenda Foundation can help participants:

  • Build a strong professional network
  • Gain practical international experience
  • Strengthen their CV for future opportunities
  • Learn directly from experts in digital payments and financial inclusion
  • Contribute to meaningful development initiatives impacting millions of Africans

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the AfricaNenda Foundation Program Support Internship is:

29 May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare strong application materials highlighting their interest in financial inclusion, development, and digital transformation in Africa.

Please send in your CV highlighting relevant skills and experiences, including three referees
through the applications@africanenda.org email by 29th of May 2026. Female candidates are
highly encouraged to apply.

Final Thoughts

The AfricaNenda Foundation Paid Program Support Internship 2026 is an excellent opportunity for young Africans seeking meaningful professional experience in financial inclusion and digital payments. With remote flexibility, paid support, and exposure to continental projects, this internship offers a strong platform for career growth in development, fintech, and innovation ecosystems.

Young professionals passionate about Africa’s digital future should strongly consider applying before the deadline.

For more information about this internship visit here. 

Discover more internship opportunities on OFY on here 

The post AfricaNenda Foundation Paid Program Support Internship 2026: Remote Opportunity for Young Professionals Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Drone Journalism Course by Academy.AFRICA: Learn Aerial Storytelling, Drone Reporting, and Media Innovation for Modern Journalism
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
In today’s rapidly evolving digital media landscape, journalists and content creators are increasingly embracing innovative technologies to tell deeper, more immersive stories. One of the most transformative technologies reshaping journalism is drone technology. Recognizing this growing need, Academy.AFRICA has launched a comprehensive Drone Journalism course designed to equip journalists, storytellers, activists, and media professionals with […]

The post Drone Journalism Course by Academy.AFRICA: Learn Aerial Storytelling, Drone Reporting, and Media Innovation for Modern Journalism appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
In today’s rapidly evolving digital media landscape, journalists and content creators are increasingly embracing innovative technologies to tell deeper, more immersive stories. One of the most transformative technologies reshaping journalism is drone technology. Recognizing this growing need, Academy.AFRICA has launched a comprehensive Drone Journalism course designed to equip journalists, storytellers, activists, and media professionals with practical skills in aerial reporting and drone-based storytelling.

This online course introduces learners to the world of drone journalism by combining technical drone operations with modern newsroom applications, ethical considerations, and storytelling techniques. Developed under the broader mission of Code for Africa, the course empowers participants to use drone technology responsibly and creatively to enhance investigative journalism, documentaries, news reporting, and community storytelling.

The course is ideal for individuals interested in modern digital reporting methods and those who want to create visually compelling content using aerial footage.

What is Drone Journalism?

Drone journalism refers to the use of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), commonly known as drones, in reporting, storytelling, photography, filmmaking, mapping, and investigative journalism.

Drone technology enables journalists to:

  • Capture aerial visuals from difficult-to-access locations
  • Cover environmental and humanitarian crises
  • Produce immersive documentary footage
  • Investigate infrastructure and urban development
  • Enhance storytelling with dynamic visual perspectives
  • Conduct public-interest reporting using aerial data collection

As digital storytelling evolves, drone journalism has become an increasingly valuable skill for media professionals around the world.

About the Drone Journalism Course

The Drone Journalism course offered by Academy.AFRICA provides learners with foundational and practical knowledge on how drones can be integrated into journalism and multimedia storytelling.

The course combines theoretical instruction with practical newsroom applications, helping learners understand both the technical and editorial aspects of drone reporting.

Participants will explore:

  • Drone operations
  • Pre-flight preparation
  • Regulatory compliance
  • Drone safety procedures
  • Ethical journalism practices
  • Aerial storytelling techniques
  • Newsroom integration strategies
  • Investigative journalism applications

The course also examines real-world examples of drone journalism to help learners understand how aerial footage can strengthen visual storytelling and audience engagement.

Learning Outcomes

By completing the Drone Journalism course, participants will gain several valuable skills and competencies.

1. Understanding Drone Journalism

Learners will:

  • Define drone journalism and its role in modern media
  • Understand how drones are transforming storytelling
  • Explore the future of aerial reporting in digital journalism

2. Drone Operations and Equipment Management

Participants will learn:

  • How to manage drone equipment
  • Pre-flight planning and preparation
  • Drone handling techniques
  • Safety protocols and risk management
  • Regulatory and legal compliance procedures

3. Newsroom Integration

The course teaches participants how to:

  • Integrate drone technology into newsroom operations
  • Coordinate drone journalism teams
  • Use drone footage in editorial workflows
  • Enhance production quality through aerial visuals

4. Storytelling and Investigative Reporting

Participants will develop skills in:

  • Creating immersive news stories
  • Producing documentaries using aerial footage
  • Combining traditional reporting with drone visuals
  • Conducting investigative journalism projects

5. Ethical and Legal Considerations

One of the most important sections of the course focuses on:

  • Privacy laws
  • Permissions and regulations
  • Responsible drone usage
  • Ethical storytelling practices
  • Safety considerations for drone journalists

Course Curriculum

The Drone Journalism course includes several structured learning modules.

Module 1: Introduction to Drone Journalism

This module introduces participants to:

  • The history and evolution of drone journalism
  • Drone applications in media
  • Global trends in aerial reporting

Module 2: Drone Operations

Participants will explore:

  • Drone controls and navigation
  • Flight planning
  • Equipment setup
  • Technical operations
  • Safety procedures

Module 3: Drone Journalism in the Newsroom

This module focuses on:

  • Editorial integration
  • Story production workflows
  • Collaboration within media teams
  • Multimedia storytelling strategies

Final Exam and Course Completion

Learners will complete:

  • Pre-course surveys
  • Post-course surveys
  • Final examination
  • Course summary exercises

Successful completion helps learners strengthen their professional media and digital storytelling capabilities.

Who Should Take This Course?

The Drone Journalism course is suitable for a wide range of professionals and learners interested in digital media innovation.

Ideal Participants Include:

  • Journalists
  • Media professionals
  • Documentary filmmakers
  • Content creators
  • Investigative reporters
  • Advocacy organizations
  • Civil society groups
  • NGOs
  • Educators
  • Researchers
  • Multimedia storytellers

The course is especially beneficial for professionals seeking to integrate innovative technologies into journalism and public-interest storytelling.

Benefits of the Course

Enrolling in the Drone Journalism course offers numerous professional advantages.

Key Benefits Include:

  • Practical understanding of drone journalism
  • Exposure to modern digital storytelling techniques
  • Knowledge of legal and ethical drone use
  • Skills in aerial photography and videography
  • Enhanced newsroom production capabilities
  • Improved investigative reporting skills
  • Ability to create immersive multimedia content
  • Greater competitiveness in the media industry

Learn from Industry Expert Johnny Miller

The course instructor is Johnny Miller, a globally recognized photographer, filmmaker, artist, and drone journalist based in South Africa and the United States.

Johnny Miller is also the co-founder of africanDRONE, a pan-African initiative focused on using drones for public-interest storytelling and mapping.

His work has appeared in internationally respected platforms including:

  • National Geographic
  • Time Magazine
  • Photo Art Basel
  • Unseen exhibitions

He is also affiliated with prestigious organizations and initiatives such as:

  • Code for Africa
  • London School of Economics
  • UN-Habitat

Participants in this course gain valuable insights from his extensive experience in drone storytelling and public-interest media innovation.

About Academy.AFRICA

Academy.AFRICA is an educational initiative by Code for Africa, Africa’s largest network of civic technology and data journalism laboratories.

The platform offers training opportunities designed to strengthen:

  • Journalism
  • Civic technology
  • Investigative reporting
  • Data journalism
  • Digital storytelling
  • Community empowerment

All course content is released under a Creative Commons Attribution License, encouraging knowledge-sharing and community empowerment across Africa and beyond.

Why Drone Journalism Matters Today

As audiences increasingly consume visual and multimedia content, drone journalism has become an essential skill for modern storytellers.

Drone footage enables journalists to:

  • Reveal hidden perspectives
  • Improve audience engagement
  • Provide visual evidence
  • Strengthen investigative reporting
  • Produce impactful humanitarian and environmental stories

For journalists and creators looking to stay competitive in the evolving media industry, drone journalism represents a powerful and future-oriented skill set.

How to Access the Course

Interested learners can access the course through the official Academy.AFRICA learning platform.

Official Links

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Short Courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Drone Journalism Course by Academy.AFRICA: Learn Aerial Storytelling, Drone Reporting, and Media Innovation for Modern Journalism appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Investigative Journalism Fellowship with Reuters and Durham University in London, New York, Sydney, or Toronto
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship 2027 is now officially announced, offering one of the most prestigious opportunities for early-career investigative journalists worldwide. Hosted by Durham University in partnership with Reuters, this highly competitive international fellowship gives selected candidates the opportunity to spend nine months working on a major investigative journalism project while receiving mentorship […]

The post Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Investigative Journalism Fellowship with Reuters and Durham University in London, New York, Sydney, or Toronto appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship 2027 is now officially announced, offering one of the most prestigious opportunities for early-career investigative journalists worldwide. Hosted by Durham University in partnership with Reuters, this highly competitive international fellowship gives selected candidates the opportunity to spend nine months working on a major investigative journalism project while receiving mentorship from world-class editors and researchers.

The fellowship honors the legacy of legendary journalist Sir Harry Evans, renowned for his commitment to fact-based journalism, investigative reporting, and editorial excellence. Through this initiative, talented journalists from across the world are empowered to deepen their investigative reporting skills, conduct impactful research, and contribute meaningfully to global journalism.

Applications for the 2027 Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship will open on 6 May 2026 and close on 10 July 2026.

About the Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship

The Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship is designed to support exceptional early-career journalists who demonstrate strong investigative potential, critical thinking skills, and dedication to factual reporting.

The selected fellow will spend nine months embedded inside a Reuters newsroom in one of the following global cities:

  • London
  • New York
  • Sydney
  • Toronto

During the fellowship period, the fellow will undertake an investigative journalism project while collaborating with experienced Reuters journalists and editors. In addition to newsroom exposure, the fellow will benefit from academic support and research resources provided by Durham University’s Institute of Advanced Studies (IAS).

The program combines practical newsroom experience with academic rigor, creating a unique environment where investigative journalism and research excellence intersect.

Fellowship Benefits

The Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship 2027 offers a highly attractive financial and professional package for the successful applicant.

Financial Benefits

Selected fellows will receive:

  • A monthly salary of approximately £4,444
  • Equivalent annual pro-rata salary of approximately £53,333
  • A monthly living stipend of £1,250
  • A one-time payment of £1,800 for travel and related expenses

These benefits are designed to ensure fellows can fully focus on their investigative work without financial pressure.

Professional and Academic Advantages

Participants in the fellowship will gain:

1. Mentorship from Reuters Editors

Fellows will work closely with top Reuters editors and investigative journalists, gaining hands-on newsroom experience and expert guidance.

2. Access to Durham University Research Resources

Fellows will have access to the extensive academic and research infrastructure at Durham University, particularly through the Institute of Advanced Studies.

3. Development of Investigative Reporting Skills

The fellowship emphasizes:

  • Fact-based journalism
  • Research methodology
  • Data-driven reporting
  • Investigative storytelling
  • Ethical journalism practices

4. International Exposure

The fellowship provides global newsroom exposure and networking opportunities with professionals from different countries and media environments.

5. Public Seminar Opportunity

The selected fellow will deliver a public seminar at Durham University’s Institute of Advanced Studies, sharing lessons learned and contributing to academic discussions on journalism.

Legacy and Impact of the Fellowship

The Sir Harry Evans Fellowship has already produced remarkable success stories.

The inaugural Sir Harry Evans Fellow, Waylon Cunningham, participated in a Reuters investigative team that won the 2024 Pulitzer Prize in National Reporting.

Other previous fellows include:

  • Waylon Cunningham (USA)
  • Rosa Furneaux (United Kingdom)
  • Pete McKenzie (New Zealand)

The fellowship continues to build a strong international network of investigative journalists making impactful contributions to the media industry.

Eligibility Criteria

The fellowship is open globally and welcomes applications from eligible candidates regardless of nationality or current country of residence.

Applicants should generally meet the following requirements:

  • Have between two and five years of professional journalism experience
  • Demonstrate strong investigative reporting potential
  • Show commitment to factual, ethical journalism
  • Possess excellent research and storytelling abilities

Applicants from Related Fields May Also Apply

The fellowship may also consider applicants who are not currently journalists but have relevant investigative experience in related professional fields, including:

  • Authors working on investigative projects
  • Researchers producing investigative work
  • Documentary creators
  • Photojournalists
  • Video researchers
  • Exhibition developers focusing on investigative themes

Applications from such candidates will be reviewed on a case-by-case basis.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

The Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship strongly encourages applications from underrepresented groups in journalism.

Reuters and Durham University aim to support a diverse new generation of investigative journalists and newsroom leaders from across the globe.

Placement Locations and Immigration Requirements

The final placement city will be determined by the Fellowship Committee.

Selected fellows must meet the applicable immigration and visa requirements for their assigned location.

Possible placement cities include:

  • London (United Kingdom)
  • New York (United States)
  • Sydney (Australia)
  • Toronto (Canada)

Important Application Rules

Applicants should carefully note the following:

  • AI-generated proposals will be disqualified
  • Late applications will not be accepted
  • Applications must be submitted before the official deadline

Candidates are encouraged to prepare original and authentic investigative project proposals.

Application Timeline

Application Opening Date

6 May 2026 at 9:00 AM BST

Application Deadline

10 July 2026 at 12:00 Noon BST

Applications submitted after the deadline will not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their application through the official Durham University fellowship portal.

Applicants are also encouraged to review the Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) before applying.

About Reuters

Reuters is the news and media division of Thomson Reuters and is recognized as one of the world’s largest multimedia news providers.

Reuters partnered with Durham University to establish this landmark initiative in honor of Sir Harry Evans, who previously served as Editor-at-Large at Reuters.

Statements from Fellowship Leaders

According to acclaimed journalist and Sir Harry Evans’ widow, Tina Brown:

“I know that Harry would have wanted to hire Waylon in a heartbeat.”

Reuters Editor-in-Chief Alessandra Galloni also emphasized Reuters’ commitment to supporting diverse investigative journalists and newsroom leaders through this fellowship.

Why You Should Apply

The Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship stands out as one of the best international journalism fellowships available for early-career investigative reporters because it combines:

  • Competitive funding
  • Global newsroom access
  • Professional mentorship
  • Academic research support
  • International networking opportunities
  • Prestigious institutional partnerships

For aspiring investigative journalists seeking global recognition and high-level professional growth, this fellowship represents a career-transforming opportunity.

 

Eligible Experience Level

Applicants should generally have:

  • Between 2 and 5 years of professional experience in journalism or investigative-related work

Application Deadline

10 July 2026

Official Links

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Sir Harry Evans Global Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Investigative Journalism Fellowship with Reuters and Durham University in London, New York, Sydney, or Toronto appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Montgomery Community Media Summer 2026 Opportunities in the USA: Internships, Fellowship, and Media Jobs for Journalists, Creators, and Digital Storytellers
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Montgomery Community Media has officially announced multiple exciting opportunities for Summer 2026, including internships, fellowships, and part-time media positions for aspiring journalists, digital storytellers, content creators, production technicians, and multimedia professionals. These opportunities are designed for individuals passionate about journalism, community storytelling, media production, broadcast news, podcasting, video editing, digital engagement, and equitable representation in […]

The post Montgomery Community Media Summer 2026 Opportunities in the USA: Internships, Fellowship, and Media Jobs for Journalists, Creators, and Digital Storytellers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Montgomery Community Media has officially announced multiple exciting opportunities for Summer 2026, including internships, fellowships, and part-time media positions for aspiring journalists, digital storytellers, content creators, production technicians, and multimedia professionals.

These opportunities are designed for individuals passionate about journalism, community storytelling, media production, broadcast news, podcasting, video editing, digital engagement, and equitable representation in media. Based in Montgomery County, Maryland, Montgomery Community Media (MCM) continues to empower local voices through training, mentorship, media access, and inclusive storytelling initiatives.

The organization is actively recruiting interns, fellows, production technicians, and teaching assistants to support its growing media and community engagement programs.

About Montgomery Community Media (MCM)

Montgomery Community Media is a nonprofit media organization committed to strengthening community voices through accessible multimedia tools, education, storytelling, and public-interest journalism.

MCM serves more than one million residents of Montgomery County and promotes:

  • Inclusive community media
  • Public access television
  • Journalism education
  • Digital storytelling
  • Youth media empowerment
  • Community engagement
  • First Amendment values
  • Public-interest telecommunications

The organization strongly values diversity, equity, and inclusion and encourages applications from people of all backgrounds and experiences.

Summer 2026 Opportunities at Montgomery Community Media

The organization is currently offering several opportunities across journalism, production, digital storytelling, and media education.

The available opportunities include:

  1. Part-Time Production Technician
  2. Media Production Internship – Summer 2026
  3. Digital Futures Institute Fellowship – Summer 2026
  4. Broadcast News Internship – Summer 2026
  5. Part-Time Teaching Assistant
  6. Part-Time Production Technicians

Each opportunity provides practical experience, mentorship, professional networking, and hands-on learning within a real media environment.

Part-Time Production Technician

Position Overview

The Part-Time Production Technician position is ideal for individuals with strong technical production skills and experience in both field and studio environments.

Production Technicians play an important role in supporting high-quality productions for broadcast television, digital media projects, and community programming.

Work Schedule

The selected candidate will work:

  • Monday
  • Tuesday
  • Thursday
  • Friday
  • 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM

Candidates should also be available for occasional evening and weekend assignments.

Key Responsibilities

Selected technicians will:

  • Support studio and field productions
  • Operate Electronic News Gathering (ENG) cameras
  • Use professional video editing equipment
  • Assist with interviews and news package production
  • Set up and dismantle production staging materials
  • Operate control room equipment
  • Coordinate studio productions
  • Run graphics and production software
  • Support production crews and journalists

Preferred Qualifications

Applicants are expected to demonstrate:

  • Experience with studio and field production
  • Adobe Creative Cloud editing skills
  • Understanding of cinematography and lighting
  • Experience with broadcast standards
  • Strong teamwork and customer service abilities
  • Commitment to community media and public-interest journalism
  • Valid driver’s license and clean driving record

Physical Requirements

Candidates should be able to:

  • Lift and carry up to 40 pounds
  • Stand for extended periods
  • Bend, twist, stretch, and move equipment frequently

Salary

The hourly pay for this role ranges between:

  • $23.09 – $28.14 per hour

Deadline

Applications must be submitted by:

  • 22 May 2026 at 5:00 PM ET

How to Apply

Applicants should send their resume to:

  • hiring@mymcmedia.org

Media Production Internship – Summer 2026

Internship Overview

The Media Production Internship offers college and high school students an excellent opportunity to gain hands-on experience in media production and digital storytelling.

Interns will work directly with experienced media professionals while learning podcast production, television production, camera operations, editing, lighting, audio production, and social media storytelling.

Internship Details

Location

  • Silver Spring, Maryland, USA

Work Format

  • Onsite with limited hybrid opportunities

Weekly Hours

  • 15 hours per week

Internship Dates

  • Start Date: 29 June 2026
  • End Date: 21 August 2026

Responsibilities

Interns will:

  • Create social media content
  • Produce short-form videos and reels
  • Conduct interviews with community leaders
  • Assist with scripting and storyboarding
  • Support live event productions
  • Capture behind-the-scenes media content
  • Research community stories
  • Assist with the YouthTalk podcast
  • Organize footage for post-production

Preferred Qualifications

Ideal applicants include students with:

  • Passion for media and community engagement
  • Strong communication skills
  • Basic knowledge of social media platforms
  • Interest in journalism or storytelling
  • Canva and video editing skills
  • Organizational and teamwork abilities

Application Process

Applicants should send:

  • Resume
  • Cover Letter

Applications should be submitted to:

  • ece@mymcmedia.org

Digital Futures Institute Fellowship – Summer 2026

Fellowship Overview

One of the most impactful opportunities available at Montgomery Community Media is the Digital Futures Institute Fellowship.

This 12-week immersive fellowship focuses on amplifying diverse voices and promoting equitable representation in digital media and storytelling.

The fellowship supports emerging media creators who identify as:

  • LGBTQIA+
  • Black
  • Indigenous
  • People of Color (BIPOC)

Fellows will work collaboratively in small teams to create hyper-local stories focused on Montgomery County communities.

Fellowship Duration

  • Orientation: Mid-May 2026
  • Program Period: 1 June – 21 August 2026
  • Fellowship Reception: Final week of August 2026

Fellowship Benefits

Participants will receive:

1. Professional Media Training

The fellowship includes comprehensive digital media and storytelling training valued at:

  • Approximately $8,200 per participant

2. Fellowship Stipend

Each fellow will receive:

  • $1,000 stipend

3. Networking Opportunities

Fellows will connect with:

  • Journalists
  • Media professionals
  • Storytellers
  • Community leaders
  • Creative changemakers

4. Public Visibility

Participants will showcase their projects through:

  • Public screenings
  • Digital platforms
  • Community engagement initiatives

Responsibilities

Fellows will:

  • Produce digital storytelling content
  • Participate in journalism ethics training
  • Use emerging media technologies
  • Collaborate with mentors and peers
  • Engage with community outreach activities

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Interest in digital storytelling or journalism
  • Editing and production experience
  • Commitment to racial equity and inclusion
  • Strong communication and teamwork skills
  • Ability to dedicate 20 hours weekly

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • Resume or CV
  • Cover Letter
  • Portfolio or work samples

Deadline

Applications close on:

  • 22 May 2026

Submission Email

  • ece@mymcmedia.org

Broadcast News Internship – Summer 2026

Internship Overview

The Broadcast News Internship provides practical newsroom experience for students interested in television journalism and community news production.

Interns will rotate through newsroom operations and contribute to real broadcast reporting projects.

Internship Details

Work Format

  • Hybrid (remote, fieldwork, and office-based)

Weekly Hours

  • 12–16 hours

Compensation

  • Unpaid
  • Academic credit available

Responsibilities

Interns will:

  • Participate in editorial planning
  • Conduct research and script writing
  • Shadow reporters and producers
  • Pitch news stories
  • Cover local government and community events
  • Gain on-camera experience
  • Support newsroom operations

Preferred Qualifications

Applicants should have:

  • Journalism or communications background
  • Strong storytelling skills
  • Interest in broadcast journalism
  • Knowledge of video editing tools
  • Social media storytelling experience

Bilingual skills, especially Spanish, are highly valued.

Application Materials

Applicants should submit:

  • Resume
  • Cover letter
  • Writing or broadcast samples
  • Three original Montgomery County story ideas

Submission Email

  • web@mymcmedia.org

Why These Opportunities Matter

The Summer 2026 opportunities at Montgomery Community Media provide participants with:

  • Real-world newsroom experience
  • Professional mentorship
  • Portfolio development
  • Networking opportunities
  • Exposure to multimedia storytelling
  • Community engagement experience
  • Journalism ethics training
  • Digital media production skills

Whether applicants are aspiring journalists, video producers, editors, or digital storytellers, these programs offer valuable pathways into the media industry.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

Montgomery Community Media strongly emphasizes diversity, equity, and inclusion across all programs and employment opportunities.

The organization welcomes applicants from all backgrounds and actively supports underrepresented communities in media.

MCM is also an Equal Opportunity Employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, nationality, or other protected characteristics.

Official Website

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Montgomery Community Media Summer 2026 Opportunities in the USA: Internships, Fellowship, and Media Jobs for Journalists, Creators, and Digital Storytellers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition 2026: Win Up to $50,000 Funding for Your Agribusiness Startup in Africa
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Young African entrepreneurs working to transform the continent’s food systems now have an incredible opportunity to gain funding, mentorship, recognition, and continental exposure through the GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition 2026. Organized under Generation Africa by AGRA, the competition is one of Africa’s most prestigious agribusiness entrepreneurship platforms dedicated to empowering innovative young agripreneurs across all […]

The post GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition 2026: Win Up to $50,000 Funding for Your Agribusiness Startup in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young African entrepreneurs working to transform the continent’s food systems now have an incredible opportunity to gain funding, mentorship, recognition, and continental exposure through the GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition 2026. Organized under Generation Africa by AGRA, the competition is one of Africa’s most prestigious agribusiness entrepreneurship platforms dedicated to empowering innovative young agripreneurs across all 55 African Union member states.

With a total prize pool of $160,000, the GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition supports scalable, impact-driven food and agribusiness ventures led by young Africans aged 18 to 35. The initiative seeks bold entrepreneurs developing solutions in agriculture, food security, climate resilience, nutrition, job creation, logistics, agri-tech, processing, and related sectors.

Applications are now officially open, and eligible entrepreneurs from across Africa are encouraged to apply before the June 28, 2026 deadline.

About the GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition 2026

The GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition was created to identify, celebrate, and support Africa’s most promising youth-led agribusiness ventures. Over the years, the competition has evolved into a leading continental platform for young innovators working to reshape Africa’s agricultural and food systems.

The competition recognizes that Africa’s future depends heavily on innovative agricultural enterprises that can create jobs, strengthen food systems, improve nutrition, drive climate adaptation, and promote inclusive economic growth.

For more than six years, GoGettaz has spotlighted outstanding agripreneurs from across the continent, providing them with funding opportunities, international visibility, expert mentorship, and access to influential networks.

The competition culminates in a live grand finale pitch competition at the Africa Food Systems Forum (AFSF) in Kigali, Rwanda, where finalists present their businesses before top judges, policymakers, investors, and industry leaders.

Why the GoGettaz Competition Matters for Africa’s Future

Agriculture remains one of the largest sectors in Africa, employing millions of people and serving as a major pillar of economic growth and food security. However, the continent also faces major challenges including:

  • Food insecurity
  • Climate change impacts
  • Unemployment
  • Weak agricultural value chains
  • Limited access to financing
  • Post-harvest losses
  • Low agricultural productivity

Young entrepreneurs are increasingly developing innovative solutions to these challenges through technology, climate-smart agriculture, digital tools, processing systems, and sustainable food enterprises.

The GoGettaz competition exists to accelerate these solutions by investing in Africa’s next generation of agribusiness leaders.

What the GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition Offers

The competition offers far more than just prize money. Participants gain access to visibility, networks, mentorship, training, and opportunities that can significantly scale their businesses.

1. Grand Prize Awards

The competition awards:

  • $50,000 to one male-led agribusiness
  • $50,000 to one female-led agribusiness

These grand prizes are designed to help entrepreneurs scale operations, improve infrastructure, expand markets, and increase impact.

2. Impact Awards

An additional $60,000 in impact awards will be distributed to businesses demonstrating excellence in specific categories.

Each impact award winner receives $15,000.

Impact Award Categories

Technology & Innovation

Businesses leveraging technology to transform food systems and agriculture.

Nutrition & Food Security

Solutions improving food availability, nutrition, and healthy diets.

Job Creation

Businesses generating employment opportunities, especially for youth and women.

Climate Resilience

Enterprises promoting sustainability, climate adaptation, and environmental resilience.

Gender Equity

Businesses empowering women and promoting gender inclusion.

3. Mentorship and Expert Guidance

Participants gain access to:

  • Food systems experts
  • Agribusiness mentors
  • Industry professionals
  • Development partners
  • Investors and ecosystem builders

This mentorship helps entrepreneurs refine business models, improve scalability, and strengthen market positioning.

4. Continental and Global Visibility

GoGettaz finalists receive significant exposure through:

  • Media coverage
  • International networking
  • Investor visibility
  • High-level events
  • Africa Food Systems Forum participation

This visibility often opens doors to:

  • Partnerships
  • Additional funding
  • Accelerator programmes
  • International opportunities

5. Pitch Training for Finalists

Top finalists participate in advanced pitch preparation sessions designed to strengthen:

  • Public speaking
  • Investor pitching
  • Business storytelling
  • Presentation delivery
  • Strategic communication

The training includes both virtual and in-person coaching between July and August 2026.

Types of Businesses Eligible for the Competition

The GoGettaz competition welcomes businesses from all sectors within Africa’s agrifood ecosystem.

Eligible sectors include but are not limited to:

Agricultural Production

  • Crop farming
  • Livestock
  • Fisheries
  • Poultry
  • Aquaculture

Agro-Processing

  • Food manufacturing
  • Packaging
  • Value addition
  • Food preservation

Agri-Tech and Digital Innovation

  • Digital agriculture
  • AI solutions
  • Farm management systems
  • Mobile agricultural platforms

Logistics and Distribution

  • Supply chain innovation
  • Transportation
  • Cold chain systems
  • Storage solutions

Sustainability and Climate Innovation

  • Renewable agriculture
  • Climate-smart farming
  • Sustainable inputs
  • Waste reduction

Food Security and Nutrition

  • Nutrition-focused enterprises
  • Affordable healthy food solutions
  • Community food systems

The competition encourages bold and scalable ventures capable of creating measurable impact.

Detailed Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet all eligibility requirements at the time of submission.

1. Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • Between 18 and 35 years old

2. Nationality Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Hold nationality from an African Union member state
  • Possess official identification proving nationality

The competition is open across all 55 African Union member countries.

3. Business Location Requirement

The venture must:

  • Be headquartered in an African Union member state

4. Business Registration Requirement

Applicants must ensure that:

  • Their venture is legally registered in an AU member state
    OR
  • Will be officially registered by June 28, 2026

5. Founder Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be the founder or co-founder of the business
  • Play an active leadership and operational role

6. Ethical and Legal Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Have no criminal record involving corruption, tax evasion, financial misconduct, or felony offences

Competition Timeline and Selection Process

The GoGettaz competition follows a multi-stage evaluation process.

Stage 1: Applications Open

  • Opening Date: May 18, 2026

Entrepreneurs can submit their applications online through the official portal.

Stage 2: Application Deadline

  • Deadline: June 28, 2026
  • Time: 11:59 PM EAT

Late applications will not be accepted.

Stage 3: Review and Shortlisting Process

The selection process includes multiple competitive stages:

Top 100 Selection

Applications are reviewed, and the top 100 ventures are announced.

Top 40 Selection

Outstanding businesses progress to the next round.

Top 24 Selection

The competition narrows to top-performing entrepreneurs.

Semi-Final Interviews

Selected candidates undergo interviews with evaluators and judges.

Top 12 Finalists

The best ventures are selected as finalists.

Stage 4: Pitch Training

Finalists receive comprehensive training between July and August 2026.

Training covers:

  • Pitching
  • Presentation strategy
  • Investor engagement
  • Communication skills

Stage 5: Grand Finale in Kigali, Rwanda

The live final pitch competition takes place during the Africa Food Systems Forum (AFSF) in Kigali, Rwanda in September 2026.

Finalists present their businesses before a distinguished jury panel.

Stage 6: Winners Announcement

Winners are officially announced during the Presidential Youth Town event at AFSF Kigali 2026.

Previous Winners and Success Stories

Over the years, the competition has supported remarkable African entrepreneurs.

Some past winners include:

Samuel Muyita – Uganda

Founder of Karpolax and winner of the 2025 Male Grand Prize.

Fatima El Khou – Morocco

Founder of JAFIFE and winner of the 2024 Female Grand Prize.

Dr. Iddi Mohammed Faried – Ghana

Founder of Kodu Technology and winner of the 2024 Male Grand Prize.

Andriatsitohaina Hasina Henintsoa – Madagascar

Founder of Mad’Arom and winner of the 2023 Female Grand Prize.

Ikenna Nzewi – Nigeria

Founder of Releaf Africa and winner of the 2023 Male Grand Prize.

These entrepreneurs have gone on to scale impactful businesses across Africa.

Why Young African Entrepreneurs Should Apply

The GoGettaz competition provides a unique combination of:

  • Funding
  • Recognition
  • Capacity building
  • Mentorship
  • Networking
  • Continental exposure

Unlike many startup competitions, GoGettaz specifically focuses on Africa’s food systems transformation and empowers young Africans to lead sustainable agricultural innovation.

For entrepreneurs working to solve real problems in agriculture and food systems, this competition can become a major launchpad for growth.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the Official GoGettaz Portal.

Applicants should prepare:

  • Business information
  • Registration details
  • Impact metrics
  • Pitch materials
  • Founder profile

Applications are free. Click HERE to access the FAQ’s and the Terms and Conditions.

Application Deadline

June 28, 2026

Time: 11:59 PM EAT

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early.

For more information about the GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition 2026, click HERE.

Conclusion

The GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition 2026 is more than a startup competition. It is a continental movement empowering young Africans to reshape the future of agriculture, food systems, climate resilience, and economic development.

With substantial funding, international visibility, mentorship, and access to influential networks, the programme offers transformational opportunities for young agripreneurs who are ready to scale innovative businesses and create lasting impact across Africa.

Young founders working in agriculture, food systems, agri-tech, sustainability, logistics, processing, and nutrition should strongly consider applying before the deadline.

Discover more global Competition and Grant opportunities on OFY.

The post GoGettaz Agripreneur Prize Competition 2026: Win Up to $50,000 Funding for Your Agribusiness Startup in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AIMS Ghana Opens Applications for Master of Mathematical Sciences for Teachers (MMST) — Fully Supported Professional Master’s for African Mathematics Educators
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The African Institute for Mathematical Sciences Ghana (AIMS Ghana) is now accepting applications for its Master of Mathematical Sciences for Teachers (MMST) program — a two-year, part-time professional master’s degree designed specifically for mathematics teachers across Africa. This program offers a unique opportunity for educators to deepen their mathematical knowledge, strengthen their teaching practice, and […]

The post AIMS Ghana Opens Applications for Master of Mathematical Sciences for Teachers (MMST) — Fully Supported Professional Master’s for African Mathematics Educators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Institute for Mathematical Sciences Ghana (AIMS Ghana) is now accepting applications for its Master of Mathematical Sciences for Teachers (MMST) program — a two-year, part-time professional master’s degree designed specifically for mathematics teachers across Africa.

This program offers a unique opportunity for educators to deepen their mathematical knowledge, strengthen their teaching practice, and gain exposure to advanced mathematical sciences, computing, and research-based pedagogy.

Applications close on 31 May 2026 at 18:00 GMT.

About the Master of Mathematical Sciences for Teachers program

The African Institute for Mathematical Sciences Master of Mathematical Sciences for Teachers is a specialized postgraduate program aimed at empowering secondary school mathematics teachers with advanced mathematical understanding and modern pedagogical skills.

It is structured to strengthen both:

  • Mathematical foundations
  • Teaching methodology and innovation

The program blends core mathematical theory with practical classroom applications, ensuring teachers can deliver high-quality, research-informed instruction in their schools.

The program is delivered:

  • Online for non-Ghanaian African participants
  • Hybrid for Ghana-based participants
  • On a part-time basis over two years

This makes it accessible to working teachers across the African continent.

Program structure (two years)

The MMST program is organized into three key phases:

1. Skills phase (foundational training)

This phase builds strong mathematical and teaching foundations. Courses include:

  • Mathematical Proofs with Logic
  • Mathematical Problem Solving
  • Calculus
  • Linear Algebra
  • Statistics for Teachers
  • Vectors and Mechanics
  • Introduction to Python
  • Introduction to Probability
  • Academic Writing and Research Methods
  • Pedagogical Theory in Mathematical Education
  • Assessment in Mathematics Education
  • Physical Problem Solving

This phase ensures teachers are grounded in both theoretical mathematics and practical teaching tools.

2. Review phase (advanced mathematical topics)

This phase exposes participants to higher-level mathematical and computational topics, including:

  • Quantum Computing
  • Real Analysis
  • Data Science
  • Financial Mathematics
  • Discrete Mathematics
  • Ordinary Differential Equations and Dynamical Systems
  • Group Theory
  • Numerical Analysis with Python
  • Statistics with R
  • Geometry
  • Number Theory
  • Vectors and Geometry

This phase strengthens analytical thinking and introduces modern applications of mathematics in science and technology.

3. Research phase

In the final phase, participants:

  • Conduct project-based research
  • Apply mathematical and pedagogical knowledge to real-world teaching challenges
  • Develop a research-informed teaching project
  • Demonstrate mastery of mathematical sciences in education

This phase is designed to bridge theory, practice, and innovation in classroom teaching.

Key highlights of the Master of Mathematical Sciences for Teachers

program

The MMST program offers several major advantages for African mathematics teachers:

  • Full scholarship (tuition fully covered)
  • Designed specifically for in-service mathematics teachers
  • Flexible learning format (online and hybrid)
  • Strong focus on mathematics, pedagogy, computing, and research
  • Exposure to advanced mathematical sciences
  • Emphasis on innovation in classroom teaching
  • Internationally trained faculty and research-informed curriculum
  • Opportunity to strengthen teaching impact across Africa

Eligibility requirements

To apply for the MMST program, candidates must meet the following criteria:

Applicants must:

  • Hold a bachelor’s degree with sufficient mathematics background
  • Be a citizen of an African country
  • Currently be teaching mathematics in an African country
    • Junior secondary school (Grades 7–9) OR
    • Senior secondary school (Grades 10–12)

This ensures the program directly strengthens active classroom educators across Africa.

Application requirements

Applicants are required to submit:

  • Updated curriculum vitae (CV)
  • Post-secondary academic transcripts and certificates
  • Consent and support letter from the head teacher of current school
  • Contact details of two referees (one must be from the current school)

All referee recommendations must be submitted through the application portal.

Application timeline

  • Application opens: 24 April 2026 (08:00 GMT)
  • Application deadline: 31 May 2026 (18:00 GMT)
  • Selection: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted

How to apply

Applications are submitted through the official AIMS portal:

Click here for more and to apply

Why this program matters

The MMST program is more than a degree — it is a capacity-building initiative designed to transform mathematics education across Africa.

Participants gain:

  • Stronger mathematical foundations
  • Modern teaching methodologies
  • Research and analytical skills
  • Exposure to computational tools like Python and R
  • Confidence to improve classroom learning outcomes

Ultimately, this program helps strengthen STEM education systems across African schools by empowering the teachers who shape future generations.

For more global Scholarship opportunities Click here.

The post AIMS Ghana Opens Applications for Master of Mathematical Sciences for Teachers (MMST) — Fully Supported Professional Master’s for African Mathematics Educators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Knight-Georgetown Institute (KGI) Hiring: Policy Analyst and Research Analyst Roles in AI, Platform Governance, and Technology Policy.
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Knight-Georgetown Institute is currently recruiting for two high-impact early-career positions: Policy Analyst Research Analyst Both roles are based in Washington, D.C. (hybrid) and focus on critical work at the intersection of artificial intelligence, platform governance, competition policy, and the future of the online information ecosystem. These positions are designed for candidates who are analytically […]

The post Knight-Georgetown Institute (KGI) Hiring: Policy Analyst and Research Analyst Roles in AI, Platform Governance, and Technology Policy. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Knight-Georgetown Institute is currently recruiting for two high-impact early-career positions:

  • Policy Analyst
  • Research Analyst

Both roles are based in Washington, D.C. (hybrid) and focus on critical work at the intersection of artificial intelligence, platform governance, competition policy, and the future of the online information ecosystem.

These positions are designed for candidates who are analytically strong, intellectually curious, and eager to contribute to influential research and policy work shaping global technology governance debates.

Application deadline for both roles: June 1, 2026

POLICY ANALYST 

The Policy Analyst role at KGI is focused on competition policy, AI governance, and digital platform regulation. It is designed for early-career professionals who want to translate complex technical research into clear, actionable policy recommendations used by governments and regulators.

The role sits at the intersection of:

  • Artificial intelligence policy
  • Competition and antitrust policy
  • Platform governance
  • Digital market regulation
  • Policy communications

The successful candidate will contribute directly to policy outputs used by decision-makers in the United States, European Union, and United Kingdom.

Key Responsibilities

1. Policy Research and Analysis

The Policy Analyst will:

  • Conduct interdisciplinary policy research across economics, law, computer science, and social science
  • Analyze competition policy and AI governance frameworks
  • Synthesize complex empirical evidence into policy recommendations
  • Produce structured policy briefs and analytical reports

2. AI and Competition Policy Work

Core thematic areas include:

  • Digital competition and antitrust policy
  • Generative AI governance frameworks
  • Platform regulation and accountability
  • Algorithmic systems and digital markets
  • Data access and governance issues

3. Policy Communication and Engagement

The role also includes:

  • Translating technical research into accessible policy language
  • Supporting policy communications for public and institutional audiences
  • Contributing to reports, briefings, and policy outputs

4. Events and Convenings

The analyst will also support:

  • Workshops with policymakers and researchers
  • Expert convenings
  • Conferences, including KGI’s Digital Competition Conference

Required Qualifications

Applicants should have:

  • Bachelor’s degree in economics, public policy, computer science, or related field
  • At least 2 years of relevant experience in policy or research
  • Strong writing and analytical skills
  • Ability to synthesize complex technical and empirical material
  • Interest in AI policy and competition regulation
  • Ability to manage multiple deadlines and projects

Preferred Qualifications

  • Graduate degree in a related field
  • Familiarity with antitrust or microeconomic concepts
  • Experience with AI governance or regulatory frameworks
  • Quantitative research experience in social science
  • Experience in policy or advocacy environments

Salary and Work Arrangement

  • Salary: $60,000 – $70,000 annually
  • Hybrid position
  • Must be based in Washington, D.C.

Application Requirements

  • CV
  • Cover letter
  • Writing sample demonstrating policy analysis ability

Click here for more and to apply.

Application Deadline

June 1, 2026

RESEARCH ANALYST 

Overview of the Research Analyst Position

The Research Analyst role is designed for early-career researchers who want to work deeply across interdisciplinary fields such as:

  • Online platforms and social media systems
  • Artificial intelligence governance
  • Technology policy and regulation
  • Digital information ecosystems

This role focuses heavily on research synthesis, academic analysis, and translating complex findings into accessible policy-relevant outputs.

Key Responsibilities

1. Research Synthesis and Analysis

The Research Analyst will:

  • Conduct literature reviews across multiple disciplines
  • Synthesize academic and policy research findings
  • Support original research projects within KGI
  • Distill complex technical content into clear insights

2. Policy and Research Communication

The role includes:

  • Writing research summaries and reports
  • Supporting policy communications for public and institutional audiences
  • Translating academic research into accessible formats
  • Contributing to briefing materials for stakeholders

3. Workshops and Convenings Support

The analyst will assist in organizing:

  • Research workshops
  • Academic-policy convenings
  • Conferences and expert discussions
  • Cross-sector engagement events

4. Cross-Disciplinary Collaboration

The role involves working across:

  • Law
  • Computer science
  • Social science
  • Public policy
  • Communications and media research

Required Qualifications

Applicants should have:

  • Bachelor’s degree in any field with relevant coursework
  • At least 2 years of research or analytical experience
  • Strong writing and critical thinking skills
  • Experience conducting literature reviews
  • Interest in AI policy or platform governance
  • Ability to collaborate across disciplines
  • Strong organizational skills

Preferred Qualifications

  • Graduate degree in a relevant field
  • Quantitative or qualitative research experience
  • Experience with tools such as R, Python, or Stata
  • Familiarity with AI systems or algorithmic governance
  • Experience in interdisciplinary research environments

Salary and Work Arrangement

  • Salary: $60,000 – $70,000 annually
  • Hybrid position
  • Must be based in Washington, D.C.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  • CV
  • Cover letter

Click here for more and to apply.

Application Deadline

June 1, 2026
